# CLAUDE.md - Backend (lzg.backend)

> AI assistant guidelines for the NestJS API backend

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-11

---

## ⚠️ Critical Priorities (Backend API)

> **See [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) for the complete priority hierarchy.** This section covers backend-specific applications of those principles.

### Backend Priority Applications

| Priority | Backend Implementation |
|----------|----------------------|
| 🔒 **Security** | JWT + CSRF on all mutations, input validation via DTOs, parameterized queries (Prisma), rate limiting, never log secrets, RBAC with AdminGuard |
| 🏗️ **Foundational** | Prisma as single DB interface, NestJS module structure, consistent error responses, proper database constraints/indexes |
| 🛡️ **Stability** | Try-catch on all async, transaction rollbacks, connection pooling, graceful shutdown, health checks |
| ✨ **UX/UI** | Fast API responses (<200ms), meaningful error messages, proper HTTP status codes, pagination on lists |
| 🎯 **Lean** | No unused endpoints, minimal dependencies, efficient queries (avoid N+1), lazy load relations |
| 🧹 **Clean** | Service/Controller separation, DTOs for all inputs, consistent naming, typed Prisma queries |
| ⚡ **Performance** | Database indexes on query fields, query optimization, connection pooling, response caching where safe |
| 📊 **Observable** | Audit logging for mutations, structured error logging, PM2 monitoring |

### Backend Checklist (Every Change)

**Quick checklist** (see `@../docs/guidelines/COMPLETION_CHECKLISTS.md` for detailed checklists):

- [ ] Protected routes have `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)`
- [ ] Admin routes have `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)`
- [ ] All inputs validated via class-validator DTOs
- [ ] User can only access their own data (no IDOR)
- [ ] No secrets in code or logs
- [ ] `npm run test` passes
- [ ] `npx prisma generate` run after schema changes

### Security Non-Negotiables

```typescript
// ✅ ALWAYS: Protected mutation endpoint
@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto, @CurrentUser() user: User) {
  // Validate ownership in service layer
}

// ✅ ALWAYS: Admin-only endpoint
@Delete(':id')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)

    11→Make golf score tracking effortless and insightful for casual golfers who value simplicity over complexity.
    12→
    13→### Mission
    14→Provide a beautiful, fast, no-nonsense platform for golfers to:
    15→- Track their rounds quickly (< 30 seconds to log a round)
    16→- Understand their progress through clear statistics
    17→- Discover new courses to play
    18→- Connect with other lazy golfers (future)

    21→> "Lazy golf for lazy golfers!"
    22→- No distractions, no bloat
    23→- Dark theme, easy on the eyes
    24→- Mobile-first design
    25→- Privacy-focused (your data is yours)

# Lazy Golfing Product Roadmap

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06
**Status:** Active Development

---

## Vision & Mission

### Vision
Make golf score tracking effortless and insightful for casual golfers who value simplicity over complexity.

### Mission
Provide a beautiful, fast, no-nonsense platform for golfers to:
- Track their rounds quickly (< 30 seconds to log a round)
- Understand their progress through clear statistics
- Discover new courses to play
- Connect with other lazy golfers (future)

### Core Value Proposition
> "Lazy golf for lazy golfers!"
- No distractions, no bloat
- Dark theme, easy on the eyes
- Mobile-first design
- Privacy-focused (your data is yours)

### Target Audience
- **Primary:** Casual golfers (play 5-20 rounds/year)
- **Secondary:** Regular golfers who want simple tracking
- **Demographics:** 25-55, tech-savvy, value minimalist design

# Technical Debt Register

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06
**Review Cadence:** Monthly

---

## Overview

Technical debt is the implied cost of additional work caused by choosing an easy/quick solution now instead of a better approach that would take longer.

### Debt Types
- **DELIBERATE:** Consciously took shortcut to ship faster
- **ACCIDENTAL:** Didn't know better at the time
- **BIT ROT:** Code degrades as dependencies/context changes

### Payoff Strategy
1. Fix critical issues immediately
2. Reserve 20% of sprint capacity for debt reduction
3. Refactor while working in area (Boy Scout Rule)
4. Don't accumulate faster than we pay down

---

## Severity Levels

| Level | Response Time | Examples |
|-------|---------------|----------|
| **Critical** | Immediate | Production down, data loss, security breach |
| **High** | Within 1 week | User-facing bugs, performance issues |

The auth module handles Google OAuth 2.0 authentication and JWT session management.

```

├── admin.guard.ts          # @UseGuards(AdminGuard) - requires 'admin' role
├── csrf.guard.ts           # @UseGuards(CsrfGuard) - double-submit pattern
├── csrf.util.ts            # CSRF token generation utilities
└── current-user.decorator.ts  # @CurrentUser() parameter decorator
```

```
1. Frontend → GET /auth/google
2. Redirect to Google consent screen
